title: Permutation counts (n!) — the combinatorial basis of proteus verse
why: A proteus verse of n freely-permutable words has n! orderings before the meter gate is applied; this counting is the engine behind Caramuel's permutational poetics. The 8-word 'Tot tibi' verse has 8! = 40320 orderings.
chiron_recovers: Feeding the factorial sequence to chiron.collapse() recovers the exact generator a(n) = n * a(n-1), verified by held-out prediction. Reproduce: python3 -c "import json,chiron; print(chiron.collapse(json.load(open('primus_extra/combinatorial_table.json'))['factorial']).model_class, chiron.collapse(json.load(open('primus_extra/combinatorial_table.json'))['factorial']).verified)"
provenance: Standard mathematical values, curated by hand. NOT OCR-extracted from the 1663 scan. Exact page-level transcription of Caramuel's own printed combinatorial tables is the deeper OCR frontier (see PRIMUS_EXPLORATION.md).
